refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these should be separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide accurate terrain data of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Develop a layered worksite representation separating terrain geometry from operational features
Produce an integrated worksite model combining terrain and feature data into a unified map
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a layered worksite representation separating terrain geometry from operational features

potential_sources: Combining terrain and operational features in a single monolithic model may create dependencies that reduce modularity
coupling_check.notes: By separating terrain geometry from operational features into layers, the design supports independent updates and reuse, reducing coupling between physical representation and operational semantics.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of data sources to populate both terrain and operational feature layers
Risk that layering approach may increase complexity in integration if interfaces are not well defined
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume layered data representations
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces and data exchange formats between terrain and operational feature layers to ensure modularity and ease of integration.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system responsible for environmental modeling and data processing, including the representation of the worksite with layered terrain and operational features.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs processed environmental data and worksite representation status to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and updates that may affect the worksite representation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication of worksite data and updates between NGHE Solution and external systems or modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the DP directly relates to environmental modeling, which is central to NGHE Solution. Minor diffusion may occur due to data sharing with Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on mobility and control, not environmental representation. High diffusion expected as worksite data would need to be shared extensively.
Moderate containment as Teleoperation may use worksite data for remote control, but does not own the representation. Significant diffusion required.
Very low containment; Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not environmental modeling. High diffusion if forced to contain DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it directly manages environmental data and worksite modeling, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential minor coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to shared use of worksite data.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution if additional unrelated functionalities are added to this module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically satisfies the FR by specifying a layered worksite representation that separates terrain geometry from operational features, directly addressing the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Minor diffusion risk exists due to shared use of worksite data with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, which may require data sharing interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) for the DP, indicating strong cohesion and appropriate ownership. Minor diffusion is noted but acceptable given the system context.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module outputs processed environmental data and worksite representation status to other modules, enabling validation against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces supports traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to minimize diffusion and maintain modularity.
Avoid adding unrelated functionalities to the NGHE Solution module to prevent overloading and maintain high cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and acceptable risk levels. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and clear allocation, with only minor diffusion risks that are manageable through monitoring and interface design. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level of decomposition to ensure clarity and control rather than further subdividing, which could introduce complexity and diffusion risks.

revise.why: Monitor and manage coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to minimize diffusion and maintain modularity; avoid adding unrelated functionalities to preserve high cohesion.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR as specified.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for the DP.
Minor diffusion risks can be managed through interface design and monitoring.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase diffusion if not managed.
Overloading the NGHE Solution module with unrelated functions could reduce cohesion.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming processed environmental data meets acceptance criteria.
Interface specifications demonstrating controlled data sharing with other modules.
